what_it_does: This file stores metadata for an image attachment tied to a design-source channel record, pointing to a one-pager image asset via a URI.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
It has an attachment id att-c0b65050.
An ISO-8601 added timestamp is present: 2026-06-22T11:31:03.261537+00:00.
There are no code symbols or executable content.
standouts: References a visual one-pager asset through an image:// URI.
Is a minimal, timestamped attachment ledger entry.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
Attachment ids use an att- prefix.
Targets use a custom image:// URI scheme.
Records addition time as an ISO-8601 timestamp with timezone.
concerns: The target URI is opaque: no file extension, dimensions, or checksum.
No caption, alt text, or descriptive context is provided.
No author or provenance beyond the timestamp is recorded.
notes: A timestamped design-source image attachment that points to a one-pager asset, containing only locator metadata and no body.
questions: Where is the referenced image physically stored?
Who added this attachment and why?
Does 'p-1' indicate page 1 of a multi-page one-pager?
purpose_vs_actual: The file serves as an attachment ledger entry for an image and does so, though it contains only a locator and timestamp rather than descriptive or provenance context.
intention_signals: To associate a design-source channel record with a specific one-pager image asset so the image can be retrieved or displayed in context.
contribution: Provides a stable, timestamped pointer from a design-source channel entry to an image asset (vi-onepager/p-1), supporting retrieval and audit of when the attachment was added.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a channel-memory attachment metadata record for an image in the design-source channel, referencing image://design-source/vi-onepager/p-1 and recording an addition timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to attach a one-pager image to a design-source channel entry for later reference or display.
